title: Render sheet frames with identity and independent positions
description:
Context:
Each sheet in Sheetspace is a movable object on the workspace. The MVP needs identifiable frames before dragging, grid editing, rename, and persistence can be useful. Broader frame features such as resizing, z-order management, collapse, and grouping are future scope.

What to do:
Render each sheet inside a visible frame at its own workspace x/y position. Include a header bar with enough identity to distinguish sheets and a body area reserved for the grid. Keep the frame surface minimal and compatible with later dragging, grid rendering, and sheet rename.

Acceptance criteria:
- Every sheet in workbook state renders as a distinct frame.
- Frames use each sheet position rather than a fixed stack or tab list.
- Frame headers display the current visible sheet name.
- Multiple frames can be visible at different positions.
- Frame rendering does not require cells to contain values.
- MVP does not add frame resizing, z-order controls, collapse/minimize/hide, or grouping.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover rendering zero, one, and multiple positioned frames, visible sheet names, and absence of grid/content requirements for frame rendering.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/sheet-frames.md, plan/features/workspace.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by workspace sheet creation.
close_reason: Completed frame rendering implementation and passed separate review

title: Implement workspace pan and zoom interactions
description:
Context:
The MVP must prove the spatial loop by letting users pan around a 2D workspace and zoom in/out. The broader workspace brief mentions viewport persistence, navigation history, reference lines, search, and minimaps, but those are not part of the MVP acceptance criteria.

What to do:
Add workspace viewport state and interactions for panning and zooming. Keep controls discoverable and testable, and ensure sheet frames remain positioned in workspace coordinates as the viewport changes. Keep the implementation scoped to pan/zoom only.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can pan the workspace.
- User can zoom the workspace in and out.
- Frame positions remain stable in workspace coordinates while viewport transforms change.
- Pan/zoom interactions do not interfere with cell editing or frame dragging once those features exist.
- Zoom limits prevent unusable extreme scales.
- MVP does not add viewport persistence, navigation history, minimap, search, command palette navigation, or formula reference lines.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover viewport state changes through available controls/events, frame rendering after pan/zoom, coordinate behavior needed by frame dragging, and zoom limit behavior. Manual test notes may cover pointer-wheel behavior if jsdom cannot fully exercise it.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/workspace.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by workspace and frame rendering.

title: Implement single-cell selection and sheet focus transitions
description:
Context:
The MVP grid only needs single-cell selection, but selection must work across multiple sheets and support committing active edits when focus moves to another cell or sheet. Error-cell selection is verified later when formula display exists.

What to do:
Add UI state and interactions for selecting one cell at a time. Track the active sheet/cell and update selection when the user clicks another cell or another sheet. Keep range, row, and column selection out of scope.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can select a single cell in a sheet.
- Selection visibly identifies the active cell.
- Selecting another cell moves selection to that cell.
- Selecting a cell in another sheet moves active sheet/cell focus.
- Empty, text, numeric-looking, and formula cells are selectable through the same path.
- Range, row, and column selection are not added in MVP.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover selecting cells, moving selection within a sheet, moving selection between sheets, and selecting cells with seeded text/formula content. Formula-error cell selection is covered by the formula display bead.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/grid-editing.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by grid rendering.
close_reason: Completed selection implementation, checks passed, reviewed

title: Recompute formula display results after workbook edits
description:
Context:
The MVP requires formula evaluation to update after committed workbook edits while preserving raw formula text for editing. Frontend recalculation is authoritative and optimistic; it does not wait for backend autosave success. Rename-triggered recomputation depends on the rename behavior existing first.

What to do:
Wire formula evaluation into committed workbook state updates so formula display values refresh after relevant changes. A full-workbook recompute is acceptable for MVP; do not introduce dependency-graph optimization unless it is already the simplest local implementation.

Acceptance criteria:
- While a cell is in edit mode, the edit text is shown and not used for formula evaluation until commit.
- Committing an input cell edit updates formula display results that depend on it.
- Committing a formula cell edit updates its own parsed/evaluated display result.
- Editing a formula back to non-formula content removes formula display behavior for that cell.
- Sheet rename triggers recomputation against current visible sheet names; formulas using old names show `#REF!`.
- Row/column append updates formula reference validity against the latest local dimensions.
- Formula errors are derived/rendered without replacing raw formula text.
- Recompute failures remain cell-level and do not prevent unrelated cells from displaying.
- No post-MVP reference visualizations or dependency graph UI are introduced.

Test expectations:
Unit or integration tests cover recompute after referenced cell edits, formula-to-value edits, value-to-formula edits, formula error updates, cross-sheet reference updates, rename-induced `#REF!`, row/column append effects on references, edit-mode non-recompute behavior, and unrelated formulas continuing after an error.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src, frontend/src/App.tsx, plan/features/formulas.md, plan/features/grid-editing.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by formula evaluator, cell editing state work, and sheet rename behavior.

title: Reconcile MVP plan wording with formula and persistence decisions
description:
Context:
The MVP plan still contains wording that predates the closed formula and persistence decision beads. In particular, `plan/mvp.task.md` says cross-sheet formula references may be supported when they fit naturally, while the closed formula decision and current MVP formula beads require cross-sheet references. The MVP plan should not contradict the active bead backlog.

What to do:
Update the MVP planning text so it reflects the recorded decisions without expanding implementation scope. Align the MVP plan with required cross-sheet SUM references, named sheet creation/rename behavior, raw formula preservation, rename-without-formula-rewrite behavior, and persistence of sheet names/renames alongside sheets, dimensions, cells, formulas, and positions.

Acceptance criteria:
- MVP plan no longer describes cross-sheet formula references as optional.
- MVP plan mentions visible sheet names where needed for cross-sheet formulas.
- MVP plan records that MVP rename does not rewrite formulas and old-name references display `#REF!` after recomputation.
- MVP persistence acceptance includes sheet ids, visible sheet names, renames, dimensions, raw cell content, raw formulas, and positions.
- The docs update does not add post-MVP features such as formula reference rewriting, reference visualization, viewport persistence, accounts, collaboration, import/export, or multiple workbooks to MVP scope.

Test expectations:
No code tests required. Review the edited MVP plan against the closed formula and persistence decision beads.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
plan/mvp.task.md, plan/features/formulas.md, plan/features/persistence.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by the recorded formula and persistence decisions.

title: Add MVP end-to-end regression coverage and final acceptance pass
description:
Context:
The MVP spans backend persistence, spatial workspace interactions, named sheet creation/rename, grid editing, cross-sheet formulas, structure changes, autosave status, and reload behavior. A final bead should verify the integrated user loop without expanding scope beyond the MVP plan.

What to do:
Add focused integration/regression coverage for the complete MVP workflow and run the project checks. Fix only issues needed to satisfy existing MVP beads and record any out-of-scope gaps as follow-up beads.

Acceptance criteria:
- Covered workflow creates multiple named sheets through the supported creation paths, arranges them, renames a sheet, edits values, enters same-sheet and cross-sheet SUM formulas, appends rows/columns, autosaves/reloads, and verifies restored raw state plus frontend-calculated display.
- Formula raw text remains verbatim through edit, autosave, and reload.
- Formula error cells remain selectable/editable during regression coverage.
- Rename does not rewrite formulas; formulas using old sheet names show `#REF!`.
- Startup load failure blocks editing with retry/error state.
- Autosave failure after successful load keeps editing available and shows app-level unsaved/error state.
- Backend and frontend tests pass.
- The root build/test workflow expected by the repo passes or any inability to run it is documented.
- No future-scope feature from the plan is required to pass MVP acceptance.

Test expectations:
Run make test and any relevant frontend/backend build commands. Add regression tests at the level best supported by the existing stack for named sheet creation, rename, cross-sheet formulas, autosave status, persistence reload, and formula-error editability.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
Makefile, frontend/src/App.test.tsx, frontend/src, backend/src/test/kotlin/com/sheetspace, plan/mvp.task.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by all user-facing MVP behavior and end-to-end persistence.

title: Evaluate SUM formulas with isolated cell-level errors
description:
Context:
Formula cells should show evaluated results outside edit mode. Errors in one formula must not crash the app, block UI interaction, or prevent unrelated formulas from computing. Frontend formula evaluation is authoritative for MVP; backend persistence stores raw workbook state only.

What to do:
Implement formula evaluation against a frontend workbook snapshot for the MVP parser output. SUM should aggregate numeric values from same-sheet and cross-sheet references/ranges according to the recorded strict numeric semantics. Return typed display results for successes and cell-level errors. Do not add non-SUM functions, arithmetic operators, broad Excel compatibility, backend calculation, or dependency-graph optimization.

Acceptance criteria:
- SUM evaluates same-sheet individual references and ranges.
- SUM evaluates cross-sheet individual references and ranges by current visible sheet name.
- SUM accepts a variable number of arguments.
- Empty referenced cells contribute 0.
- Plain decimal/integer values with optional leading minus sign contribute numeric values after trimming.
- Non-empty non-numeric referenced text causes `#VALUE!`.
- Unsupported functions, parse failures, missing sheets, out-of-bounds references, unresolved ranges, and value issues become the correct MVP cell-level error labels.
- Referenced formula errors propagate; if several errors are referenced, the first error in deterministic test-documented order is returned.
- Direct and indirect cycles, including cross-sheet cycles, display `#CYCLE!` for participating cells.
- A failure in one formula does not stop evaluation of unrelated formulas.
- Evaluation never replaces or normalizes raw cell formula text.

Test expectations:
Unit tests cover numeric SUM, mixed references and ranges, same-sheet and cross-sheet references, quoted sheet names, empty cells, strict text behavior, rejected numeric-like formats, referenced formula errors, unrelated formula independence, missing/invalid references, direct cycles, indirect cycles, and cross-sheet cycles.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src, plan/features/formulas.md, plan/features/grid-editing.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by formula parser.

title: Define MVP workbook state model and addressing helpers
description:
Context:
The MVP needs one coherent application model for workbooks, sheets, cell content, sheet positions, dimensions, sheet names, and evaluated cell display state before UI, formulas, and persistence can be wired together. New workspaces start empty. New sheets are created only from a user-provided valid name, start as empty 10-column by 20-row grids, and can be referenced by visible sheet name in formulas.

What to do:
Define the minimal MVP workbook/sheet/cell types and helper functions for sheet creation, sheet rename validation, cell addressing, Excel-style column labels, range expansion, and row/column append operations. Keep the model scoped to MVP state and compatible with backend persistence.

Acceptance criteria:
- A new workbook can represent an empty workspace with zero sheets.
- A new sheet requires a caller-provided visible name and is not auto-created or auto-named.
- Sheet ids are stable and sheet names are non-empty and unique within the workbook.
- A new sheet has a stable id, visible name, x/y position, 10 columns, 20 rows, and no stored cell values.
- Cell content stores raw user input verbatim, including formulas beginning with =, formula casing, whitespace, and line breaks.
- Helpers convert zero-based column indexes to labels including A, B, Z, AA, and AB.
- Helpers parse/normalize A1 cell coordinates and expand rectangular ranges inside sheet bounds.
- Helpers support sheet-name lookup for cross-sheet formula resolution by current visible sheet name.
- Helpers support validating sheet create/rename attempts without rewriting formulas.

Test expectations:
Unit tests cover empty workbook defaults, create-sheet validation, duplicate/empty sheet names, rename validation, new-sheet grid defaults, column labels past Z, A1 parsing, invalid addresses, range expansion, cross-sheet sheet-name lookup, and raw formula text preservation.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src, frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src/App.test.tsx, backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace, plan/mvp.task.md, plan/features/columns-rows-structure.md, plan/features/formulas.md

Dependencies/blockers:
None. This is a foundation bead for the MVP.
close_reason: Completed MVP workbook model and addressing helpers with frontend/backend tests

title: Move sheet frames by dragging header bars
description:
Context:
Moving a sheet in the MVP happens through its frame header bar. Sheet positions must update independently so users can arrange multiple worksheets spatially, and movement should respect the workspace pan/zoom coordinate system.

What to do:
Implement pointer dragging on frame headers to update a sheet frame x/y position in workbook state. Keep body interactions available for grid selection/editing rather than frame movement. Use workspace-coordinate deltas so the behavior remains correct after pan and zoom.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dragging a frame header moves that sheet.
- Dragging one sheet does not move other sheets.
- Dragging the grid/body area does not initiate frame movement.
- Updated positions are reflected in workbook state for persistence.
- Drag movement remains correct after workspace pan and zoom.
- No resizing, z-order management, collapse/minimize/hide, grouping, or editable frame titles are introduced.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover header drag movement, body drag non-movement, independent positions for multiple frames, and drag behavior under a non-default pan/zoom transform where feasible.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/sheet-frames.md, plan/features/workspace.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by sheet frame rendering and workspace pan/zoom.

title: Render empty spatial workspace with named sheet creation
description:
Context:
The app should open to an empty 2D workspace where users can create multiple named sheets. Workspaces start empty, and sheet creation requires a user-provided non-empty unique sheet name. This replaces the skeleton health-check screen with the first usable Sheetspace surface.

What to do:
Implement the workspace shell and sheet creation flows. Provide a discoverable button path that creates at the current viewport center and a workspace right-click/context-menu path that creates at the clicked workspace coordinate. Both paths must collect and validate a sheet name before creating the sheet.

Acceptance criteria:
- App opens to a workspace surface when loaded.
- Empty workbook state displays an empty workspace, not a default sheet.
- User can create a sheet from a button/toolbar path after entering a valid unique name.
- User can create a sheet from a workspace right-click/context-menu path after entering a valid unique name.
- Button-created sheets are placed at the current viewport center.
- Context-created sheets are placed at the clicked workspace coordinate.
- Empty or duplicate sheet names are rejected with a testable validation/error state and no sheet is added.
- User can create multiple sheets with stable ids and distinguishable names.
- Newly created sheets use 10 columns, 20 rows, no cell values, and the chosen workspace position.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover empty initial render, button creation, context/right-click creation or its position calculation path, duplicate/empty name rejection, creating multiple sheets, and new-sheet defaults. Manual test notes may cover native context-menu behavior if jsdom cannot fully exercise it.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src/App.test.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/workspace.md, plan/features/sheet-frames.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by MVP workbook model.
close_reason: Completed workspace shell and named sheet creation flow; reviewed with no findings

title: Add frontend workbook API client and startup load lifecycle
description:
Context:
The UI should use backend persistence rather than local-only state. This bead provides the frontend API client surface and startup load lifecycle needed before autosave wiring can be added. MVP loads the current workbook from the backend before editing is allowed; frontend formula evaluation remains authoritative and optimistic after load.

What to do:
Add a frontend API client for the workbook load endpoint and per-entity update endpoints exposed by the backend. Wire app startup to load the workbook from the backend before editing is allowed. Implement the startup loading, retry, and blocking error states. Keep committed-change autosave queueing and app-level save status in a separate autosave bead.

Acceptance criteria:
- App startup requests the current workbook from the backend before showing an editable workspace.
- Empty backend state renders an empty workspace after successful load.
- Startup load failure shows a retry/error screen and blocks workbook editing until load succeeds.
- Retry after startup load failure can load the workbook and enter the editable workspace.
- Frontend API client exposes calls for sheet create, sheet rename, sheet position, cell content, row append, and column append updates without wiring autosave behavior in this bead.
- Backend response failures from startup load are represented in a testable app-level error state.
- Existing health-check placeholder UI is removed or superseded by MVP workspace loading behavior.
- No explicit Save button, multi-workbook, account, collaboration, import/export UI, autosave queue, durable offline queue, or per-cell save indicator is introduced.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests mock fetch for successful startup load, failed startup load and retry, empty backend state, API client request/response handling for per-entity update calls, and health-check placeholder removal/supersession. Autosave queueing and save-status tests belong to the dedicated autosave bead.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src/App.test.tsx, frontend/src, frontend/src/test/setup.ts

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by workbook API endpoints, MVP model types, the workspace shell, and the MVP persistence decision.

title: Wire frontend per-entity autosave queue and save status
description:
Context:
After the frontend can load the workbook and call backend per-entity APIs, committed MVP state changes need to autosave without blocking local editing. The MVP persistence decision defines app-level save status and per-entity queue semantics.

What to do:
Wire committed workbook changes into per-entity autosave calls for sheet create, sheet rename, sheet position, cell content, row append, and column append. Implement app-level save status and per-entity queueing: each entity key has at most one running save and one queued latest replacement; different keys may save in parallel.

Acceptance criteria:
- Committed sheet create, sheet rename, sheet position, cell content, row append, and column append changes trigger the matching per-entity API call.
- Transient in-progress cell edits do not autosave until committed.
- Autosave failures after successful load keep the in-memory workbook editable and show an app-level save-failed/unsaved state.
- Save status is app-level only, with loading handled by the startup load bead and post-load states such as saving, saved, and save failed/unsaved.
- For each entity key, a new committed change replaces any queued unsent change for that key but does not cancel a request already running.
- Different entity keys may save in parallel.
- Stale save completions must not mark newer unsaved work as saved.
- Frontend formula evaluation remains optimistic and does not wait for backend save success.
- No explicit Save button, per-cell save indicator, account, collaboration, import/export UI, multi-workbook behavior, or durable offline queue is introduced.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests mock fetch for per-entity save calls, failed autosave after successful load, app-level save status, same-key queued replacement, parallel different-key saves, stale completion not marking newer work saved, and no autosave for transient in-progress edits.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src/App.test.tsx, frontend/src, frontend/src/test/setup.ts

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by frontend startup load/client lifecycle, frame dragging, sheet rename, cell editing, row/column append behavior, and the MVP persistence decision.

title: Persist sheets, cells, dimensions, formulas, and positions end to end
description:
Context:
The MVP is only complete when created sheets, sheet names/renames, grid dimensions, raw cell contents, formulas, evaluated formula behavior, and sheet positions survive backend persistence. Backend persistence stores raw state; frontend recomputes formula results after load. Autosave queueing and save status are covered by a dedicated frontend autosave bead before this end-to-end pass.

What to do:
Verify and finish the integrated MVP persistence path so the backend stores/reloads the complete MVP workbook state and the frontend restores raw state then recomputes formula display. Include created sheets, ids/names, renames, sheet positions, row/column counts, text/numeric cell contents, and raw formulas. Keep reload behavior focused on restoring raw state and recomputing formulas in the frontend.

Acceptance criteria:
- Created sheets persist after app reload.
- Sheet renames persist after app reload.
- Moved sheet positions persist after app reload.
- Appended row and column counts persist after app reload.
- Text and numeric cell contents persist after app reload.
- Raw formula strings persist verbatim after app reload and re-display frontend-evaluated results after loading.
- Cross-sheet formulas persist as raw formula text and evaluate after reload using current visible sheet names.
- Startup load failure blocks editing with retry/error state.
- Autosave failure after successful load keeps the workbook editable and shows app-level unsaved/error state.
- No post-MVP persistence fields are required, including viewport history, frame sizes, accounts, collaboration, imports, exports, evaluated formula result storage, durable offline queues, or multiple workbooks.

Test expectations:
Frontend API integration tests or mocked end-to-end state tests cover save/reload for sheets, renames, positions, dimensions, cells, raw formulas, cross-sheet formulas, empty state, startup load failure, and post-load autosave failure. Backend per-entity save/load tests remain passing.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace, backend/src/test/kotlin/com/sheetspace, plan/features/persistence.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by frontend startup load/client lifecycle, frontend autosave queue/status wiring, frame dragging, sheet rename, formula display integration, and row/column append behavior.

title: Decide MVP persistence save behavior and storage scope
description:
Context:
This decision records the MVP persistence behavior chosen for downstream backend API, storage, frontend lifecycle, and integration beads. The implementation should not re-open these product choices while working those beads.

Decision:
- MVP persistence uses backend durability for raw workbook state. Frontend formula evaluation is authoritative; backend does not calculate formulas and does not persist evaluated formula results.
- App startup loads the current workbook from the backend before editing is allowed. If initial load fails, show a retry/error screen and block workbook editing until load succeeds. Do not assume the workbook is empty on load failure.
- A real empty database loads as an empty workspace with zero sheets.
- MVP uses autosave after committed state changes. No required Save button is part of MVP.
- Autosaved committed changes include sheet create, sheet rename, sheet position changes after frame movement, cell edit commits, row append, and column append. Transient in-progress edits are not saved until committed.
- After a successful initial load, autosave failures are non-blocking: keep current in-memory workbook state editable, keep frontend formula display optimistic, and show a visible app-level unsaved/save-failed status.
- MVP save indicator is app-level only, with states such as loading, saving, saved, and save failed/unsaved. Do not add per-cell save indicators in MVP.
- Persistence APIs should be per-entity update APIs plus a load API. They should cover cell content updates, sheet creation, sheet rename/metadata update, sheet position update, row append, and column append.
- Autosave queueing is per entity key. Each key has at most one running save and one queued latest replacement. If a new edit for the same key arrives while one is running, replace the queued value with the latest committed value. Different keys may save in parallel.
- Entity keys should be chosen to match persisted units: sheet id plus cell address for cell content, sheet id plus metadata/position field group for sheet changes, and sheet/workbook structural keys for row/column append and sheet creation.
- Backend storage shape is an engineering choice as long as the observable behavior and tests match this decision.
- Durable MVP fields are workbook/schema version, sheet ids, unique visible sheet names, sheet frame positions, row/column counts, and raw cell contents including raw formulas.
- Non-MVP persistence features are excluded: accounts, collaboration, import/export, multi-workbook management, viewport history, frame sizes, evaluated formula result storage, and durable offline queues across page reload.

Acceptance criteria:
- Downstream persistence beads reference this decision instead of making their own save/load semantics choices.
- Backend and frontend tests cover startup load success/failure, empty database, per-entity updates, autosave status, failed autosave after successful load, and durable MVP fields.

Test expectations:
No code test is required for this decision bead. Downstream implementation beads must add tests named in their own test expectations.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
plan/mvp.task.md, plan/features/persistence.md, backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace, frontend/src

Dependencies/blockers:
None. Decision recorded; dependent implementation beads may proceed once their remaining dependencies are satisfied.

title: Implement cell editing commit and cancel behavior
description:
Context:
Users need to edit text, numeric-looking values, formulas, and clear existing content. Enter commits active edits, moving focus commits active edits, and Escape cancels active edits.

What to do:
Add cell edit mode, input handling, and commit/cancel semantics. Store committed raw cell content in workbook state and preserve previous content when editing is canceled. Treat committed empty input as clearing the cell by removing its stored cell entry from workbook state.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can enter edit mode for a selected cell.
- Text and numeric-looking values can be typed and committed as raw cell content.
- Formula-looking values beginning with `=` can be typed and committed as raw cell content without normalization.
- Pressing Enter commits the active edit.
- Moving focus to another cell or sheet commits the active edit.
- Pressing Escape cancels the active edit and restores prior content.
- Empty committed input clears the cell by removing the stored cell entry from workbook state; the cell renders blank afterward.
- Clearing an already-empty cell leaves workbook state unchanged apart from normal edit UI state.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests with user-event cover entering edit mode, committing with Enter, committing on focus movement, canceling with Escape, editing text, editing numeric-looking values, editing raw formula text, clearing existing text/numeric/formula content, and clearing an already-empty cell.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src/App.test.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/grid-editing.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by single-cell selection.
close_reason: Completed cell editing implementation, checks passed, reviewed

title: Append rows and columns at sheet ends
description:
Context:
The MVP structure slice requires users to append rows and columns at the ends of a sheet. Full insert/delete/reorder, resizing, custom column names, and formula refactoring during structural edits are out of scope.

What to do:
Add UI controls and state updates for appending one row and one column at the end of the target sheet. Ensure headers and address helpers reflect the new dimensions and existing cell contents remain at their original coordinates.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can append one row to a sheet.
- User can append one column to a sheet.
- Appended rows/columns appear at the end, preserving existing cell contents.
- Row numbers and column labels update correctly after appends, including labels beyond Z when reached.
- Appending structure in one sheet does not change other sheets.
- MVP does not add insert/delete/reorder, row height/column width resizing, custom names, or formula reference rewriting for moved structures.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover append row, append column, preserving existing values, labels beyond initial dimensions, and independent dimensions across sheets. Tests should use real committed cell content once editing exists.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/columns-rows-structure.md, plan/features/grid-editing.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by grid rendering, MVP model append helpers, and cell editing behavior.

title: Rewrite formula sheet references when sheets are renamed
description:
Context:
MVP sheet rename deliberately does not rewrite formulas. Formulas that reference an old sheet name show `#REF!` until edited. A later improvement should preserve formulas across rename by updating sheet-qualified references safely.

What to do:
Design and implement formula reference rewriting for sheet renames after the MVP parser/evaluator is stable. The rewrite should update only references that target the renamed sheet and preserve unrelated formula text as much as practical.

Acceptance criteria:
- Renaming a sheet updates formulas that reference the old sheet name so they continue resolving to the renamed sheet.
- Rewrites handle quoted and unquoted sheet names.
- Rewrites do not alter formulas that reference other sheets or contain old-name text outside parsed references.
- The behavior is covered for same-sheet formulas, cross-sheet formulas, and formulas with parse errors.

Test expectations:
Add parser/refactor tests for quoted names, unquoted names, non-reference text, and malformed formulas. Add integration tests for rename preserving formula results.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src, plan/features/formulas.md, plan/features/sheet-frames.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Post-MVP improvement; not required for MVP acceptance.

title: Render sheet grid with row and column headers
description:
Context:
Each MVP sheet starts as an empty 10-column by 20-row grid and should display recognizable spreadsheet row and column headers.

What to do:
Render a tabular grid inside each sheet frame using workbook sheet dimensions. Show default row numbers and Excel-style column labels. Empty cells should render as editable/selectable cells without storing values unnecessarily.

Acceptance criteria:
- New sheets render 10 columns and 20 rows.
- Row headers display 1-based row numbers.
- Column headers display Excel-style labels and handle labels beyond Z when dimensions grow.
- Empty cells are visible and addressable.
- Multiple sheet grids render independently inside their frames.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover default grid size, row headers, column labels, empty cell rendering, and multiple sheet grids.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/grid-editing.md, plan/features/columns-rows-structure.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by MVP workbook model and sheet frame rendering.

title: Rename sheets with unique visible names
description:
Context:
MVP cross-sheet formulas resolve by visible sheet name, so users need a way to set and change sheet names while names remain unique. Formula text is not rewritten after rename in MVP; formula recomputation after rename is handled by the recompute bead.

What to do:
Add sheet rename interaction from the sheet frame/header or equivalent sheet identity UI. Validate proposed names before committing. On successful rename, update workbook state and visible frame identity while preserving all raw cell content verbatim.

Acceptance criteria:
- User can rename an existing sheet from sheet identity UI.
- Proposed sheet names are trimmed for validation and must be non-empty and unique within the workbook.
- Empty or duplicate rename attempts are rejected with a testable validation/error state and the old name remains active.
- Successful rename updates the visible sheet frame identity and workbook state.
- Successful rename preserves all raw cell content and does not rewrite formulas.
- Formulas using old sheet names are not modified by this bead; recomputation and resulting `#REF!` display are covered by the formula recompute/display beads.
- Rename state changes use the same workbook shape consumed by the frontend autosave bead, but autosave wiring is not implemented here.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover successful rename, empty name rejection, duplicate name rejection, old name preservation after failed rename, visible frame identity update, workbook state update, and no formula text rewrite.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/sheet-frames.md, plan/features/formulas.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by MVP workbook model and sheet frame rendering.

title: Add backend database storage for MVP workbook state
description:
Context:
The MVP requires created sheets, unique visible names, dimensions, raw cell content, formulas, and sheet positions to persist through a real backend database using a straightforward single-instance read/write path. Backend persistence stores raw workbook state only; frontend formula evaluation is authoritative.

What to do:
Add backend database setup and a persistence repository for the MVP single-workbook state. Include schema/version handling appropriate for the first persisted shape and keep the path simple for one local instance. The database technology and table/document shape may be chosen during implementation, but the externally observable behavior must match the persistence decision.

Acceptance criteria:
- Backend starts with an initialized real database, not in-memory frontend-only storage.
- Repository can load the current workbook, returning an empty workbook with zero sheets when the database is genuinely empty.
- Repository can save and reload sheets, stable sheet ids, unique visible sheet names, row/column counts, raw cell contents, raw formulas, and frame x/y positions.
- Repository supports per-entity updates needed by autosave: sheet create, sheet rename/metadata, sheet position, cell content, row append, and column append.
- Persisted data includes a version marker or clear migration hook for the first schema.
- Evaluated formula results are not persisted.
- No accounts, collaboration, import/export, multi-workbook management, durable offline queue, or non-MVP viewport history is introduced.

Test expectations:
Backend tests cover empty initial load, per-entity update round trips, durable fields, unique sheet-name persistence, formula raw text surviving persistence unchanged, evaluated result absence, and schema/version marker behavior.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
backend/build.gradle.kts, backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace, backend/src/test/kotlin/com/sheetspace, plan/features/persistence.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by the MVP workbook model bead.

title: Decide MVP formula semantics and explicit exclusions
description:
Context:
This decision records the MVP formula behavior chosen for downstream parser, evaluator, display, and persistence beads. The implementation should not re-open these product choices while working those beads.

Decision:
- Frontend formula evaluation is authoritative for MVP; the backend persists raw workbook state only.
- Formula text is stored verbatim after commit, including casing, spacing, quoting, and line breaks. Parser/evaluator code may normalize tokens in memory but must not write normalized formula text back to workbook state or persistence.
- Function names are case-insensitive for evaluation. `SUM`, `sum`, and `Sum` all work.
- Whitespace and newlines are allowed around formula separators: after `=`, around function names, parentheses, commas, colons, references, and cross-sheet separators. Whitespace inside an address token such as `A 1` remains invalid.
- `SUM` supports same-sheet and cross-sheet references. Cross-sheet syntax is Excel-like visible sheet-name syntax: `Inputs!A1`, `Inputs!A1:B3`, `'Sales Q1'!A1`, and `'Sales Q1'!A1:B3`.
- Sheet names used in formulas resolve against current visible sheet names. Missing sheets, malformed sheet references, out-of-bounds cells, and unresolved ranges produce cell-level errors.
- Sheet renames do not rewrite formula text in MVP; formulas using an old name show `#REF!` until edited.
- `SUM` is strict numeric: empty referenced cells contribute 0; plain decimal/integer cell text contributes its numeric value; non-empty non-numeric text in a referenced cell causes `#VALUE!`.
- Plain numeric formats only: trim leading/trailing whitespace and accept integers/decimals with optional leading minus sign, such as `12`, `-3`, `4.5`, and `0.25`. Reject comma separators, currency, percentages, dates, and scientific notation as text for formula purposes.
- Referenced formula errors propagate. If multiple referenced errors are encountered, return the first error in deterministic evaluation order documented by tests.
- MVP error labels are `#PARSE!`, `#NAME!`, `#REF!`, `#VALUE!`, and `#CYCLE!`. `#PARSE!` means invalid formula syntax; `#NAME!` means unsupported/unknown function; `#REF!` means unresolved sheet/cell/range reference; `#VALUE!` means a resolved value cannot be used numerically; `#CYCLE!` means a direct or indirect formula cycle.
- Detect direct and indirect cycles, including cycles across supported cross-sheet references. Cells participating in a detected cycle display `#CYCLE!`; unrelated formulas continue computing.
- Do not add arithmetic operators, string literals, percentages, named ranges, extra functions, reference visualization UI, or optimized dependency-graph recalculation for MVP unless another bead explicitly requires them.

Acceptance criteria:
- Downstream formula beads reference this decision instead of making their own semantics choices.
- Parser/evaluator/display tests cover verbatim formula storage, case-insensitive function names, whitespace/newline tolerance, same-sheet references, cross-sheet references, strict numeric SUM behavior, error propagation, and cycle detection.

Test expectations:
No code test is required for this decision bead. Downstream implementation beads must add tests named in their own test expectations.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
plan/mvp.task.md, plan/features/formulas.md, frontend/src

Dependencies/blockers:
None. Decision recorded; dependent implementation beads may proceed once their remaining dependencies are satisfied.

title: Expose workbook load and per-entity persistence APIs
description:
Context:
The frontend needs a backend read/write path for durable MVP workbook state. Persistence uses a load API plus per-entity update APIs that match autosave keys. The backend persists raw workbook state only and does not calculate formulas.

What to do:
Add HTTP endpoints for loading the current workbook and updating MVP entities: cell content, sheet creation, sheet rename/metadata, sheet position, row append, and column append. Define request/response DTOs and validation for malformed payloads. Keep health endpoint behavior intact.

Acceptance criteria:
- Frontend can load the current persisted workbook from the backend before editing.
- Empty database state returns a valid empty workbook with zero sheets.
- Frontend can create a sheet with id/name/position through a per-entity API.
- Frontend can update raw cell content through a per-cell API.
- Frontend can rename a sheet and update sheet position through sheet-level APIs.
- Frontend can append rows and columns through structure update APIs.
- Invalid update payloads return an appropriate 4xx response without corrupting existing data.
- Backend responses provide enough success/failure information for app-level save status.
- Backend does not calculate or persist evaluated formula results.
- Health endpoint continues to work.

Test expectations:
Ktor application tests cover load-empty, create-sheet then load, cell update then load, rename then load, position update then load, row/column append then load, invalid payload handling, formula raw text persistence without evaluated results, and health endpoint regression.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace/Main.kt, backend/src/main/kotlin/com/sheetspace, backend/src/test/kotlin/com/sheetspace/ApplicationTest.kt

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by backend database storage.

title: Display formula results while preserving raw formula edits
description:
Context:
Formula cells should show evaluated results outside edit mode but reveal and preserve the stored raw formula text while editing. Stored formula text is verbatim user input, including casing, spacing, quoting, and line breaks. Formula error cells must remain selectable and editable.

What to do:
Integrate formula display results with grid rendering and editing. When a formula cell is not being edited, render its frontend-evaluated value or cell-level error. When editing, show the stored raw formula string exactly as persisted.

Acceptance criteria:
- Formula cells render evaluated SUM results outside edit mode.
- Editing a formula cell shows the stored raw formula text verbatim.
- Committing a changed formula updates the display result through frontend recomputation.
- Mixed-case, spaced, and multiline formulas retain their raw edit text while still evaluating where syntactically valid.
- Formula errors render at the cell level without crashing the app.
- Cells displaying formula errors remain selectable and editable.

Test expectations:
Frontend tests cover raw formula preservation, mixed-case formula display/editing, whitespace/newline formula preservation, evaluated display, cross-sheet formula display, error display, selecting and editing an error cell, and recompute-visible behavior after changing referenced cells.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src/App.tsx, frontend/src, plan/features/grid-editing.md, plan/features/formulas.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by recompute logic and cell editing behavior.

title: Build formula parser for SUM, A1 references, and ranges
description:
Context:
The MVP formula slice treats content beginning with = as a formula and supports SUM with same-sheet and cross-sheet A1-style cell references and 2D ranges. Formula text is stored verbatim; parsing may normalize tokens only in memory.

What to do:
Implement a small parser that recognizes formula inputs, parses SUM calls case-insensitively, parses comma-separated arguments, supports individual references and rectangular ranges, and supports Excel-like cross-sheet references using visible sheet names such as `Inputs!A1` and `'Sales Q1'!A1:B3`. Report typed parse/name/reference errors without throwing through the UI. Do not add arithmetic operators, string literals, named ranges, percentages, or additional functions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Non-formula content is ignored by the parser path.
- Formula text remains verbatim in workbook state; parser normalization does not write back to the cell.
- `SUM`, `sum`, and mixed-case variants parse as the SUM function.
- Whitespace and newlines are allowed around separators, parentheses, commas, colons, references, and after `=`, while malformed address tokens such as `A 1` remain invalid.
- Arguments can be same-sheet A1 references, same-sheet ranges, cross-sheet references, and cross-sheet ranges.
- Quoted sheet-name references parse for names with spaces or punctuation.
- Unsupported functions produce `#NAME!`; invalid syntax produces `#PARSE!`; unresolved references are represented for evaluator/display as `#REF!`.

Test expectations:
Unit tests cover valid MVP SUM examples, mixed-case function names, verbatim raw formula retention, whitespace/newline tolerance, invalid formulas, unsupported functions, invalid references, unquoted cross-sheet references, quoted cross-sheet references, and malformed quote/address cases.

Likely files or areas to inspect:
frontend/src, plan/features/formulas.md, plan/mvp.task.md

Dependencies/blockers:
Blocked by addressing and sheet-name lookup helpers from the MVP model bead.

Checks passed: make test; make compile. Review pending.

comments.text: Addressed review finding: quoted sheet names now support Excel-style escaped apostrophes, e.g. 'Owner''s Plan'!A1:B2 resolves to Owner's Plan. Added regression coverage. Checks passed: make test; make compile; git diff --check. Re-review requested.
